body.p1: A HOBC wallet stores the keys needed to receive and spend HobbyHash Coin. The blockchain records transactions, but the wallet controls the keys that authorize spending. If the keys are lost, the coins controlled by those keys can become permanently inaccessible.
body.p2: A wallet can create receiving addresses, track balances, build transactions, and broadcast payments through a synced node or wallet backend.
body.p3: A confirmation means a transaction is included in a block. More blocks after that block add more confirmations. Services may require different confirmation counts before treating a transaction as final. Confirmation policy for any specific service is To be confirmed unless that service publishes it.
body.p4: Cold storage means keeping wallet keys offline so they are not exposed to an internet-connected machine during normal use. A cold wallet can be useful for larger balances, but it requires careful backup handling and a clear process for signing transactions.
body.p5: Never share private keys. Never paste private keys into websites. If a wallet gives you a seed or recovery phrase, treat it like the wallet itself. Current public docs do not prove that every HOBC wallet build uses a recovery phrase, so follow the exact backup method shown by the wallet you are using.
hero.lead: Receive, send, back up, and protect HOBC wallet keys safely.
hero.title: Wallet Guide
list.backup.1: Encrypt wallets when the wallet software supports encryption.
list.backup.2: Use a strong password that you can store safely. If you lose the wallet password, support cannot recover it for you.
list.backup.3: If the desktop/core wallet uses a wallet.dat file, back up that file after creating or changing wallet keys.
list.backup.4: Store backups offline in more than one safe place.
list.backup.5: Never email wallet files or upload private wallet backups to random cloud links.
list.receive.1: Create a receiving address in your wallet.
list.receive.2: Give only the public receiving address to the sender or miner.
list.receive.3: Wait for the transaction to appear and gain confirmations.
list.receive.4: For mining, use the address before the dot in the worker name: HOBC_ADDRESS.WORKERNAME.
list.send.1: Check the destination address carefully before sending.
list.send.2: Send a small test amount first when using a new address or tool.
list.send.3: After broadcast, use the transaction ID in the explorer when available.
list.send.4: Do not send funds from a wallet you cannot back up or unlock.
meta.description: HOBC wallet setup, custody, backups, and security.
meta.title: Wallet Guide
notice.1: If you cannot explain your wallet backup and recovery process, do not hold larger balances yet.
notice.2: The public web wallet is custodial. For local key control, use the official Windows standalone wallet from the downloads page. Long-term holdings should use the Windows standalone wallet or other local wallet software with encrypted backups.
